Per-Cell Weighted Adjacency Score

Source: R/calculate_adjacency_score.R
adjacency_score_vec.Rd

For each cell of design_matrix, counts neighbours on rings of radius dists whose value equals the cell's own, weighted by weights. Implemented by stacking shifted copies of the matrix once per offset.

Usage

adjacency_score_vec(
  design_matrix,
  dists = c(1, 2),
  weights = c(1, 2),
  ring_type = c("manhattan", "chebyshev")
)

Arguments

design_matrix

Design matrix

dists

A vector of positive integers, ring radii to score over.

weights

Per-ring weights; must align with dists.

ring_type

Ring shape: "manhattan" (default; diamond ring) or "chebyshev" (square ring). See ring_offsets().

Value

Matrix of the same dimensions as design_matrix, where each cell holds its weighted matching-neighbour count.

On this page

Developed by Sam Rogers, Julian Taylor, Russell Edson, Wasin Pipattungsakul, University of Adelaide, Grains Research and Development Corporation.
